Agent.description: A person or organization responsible for a concept.

id.description: The concept's stable IRI. By convention it is the bundle base IRI joined with the Concept ID (the file path within the bundle, minus the `.md` suffix). Becomes the JSON-LD @id / RDF subject.

type.description: The concept's type. OKF's single required field. In LOKF the value SHOULD name a LOKF class (e.g. Metric, Dataset, Table, Playbook, GlossaryTerm); it designates the JSON-LD @type / rdf:type. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values by treating the concept as a generic lokf:Concept.

Agent.title: Agent

Citation.description: A reference to an external source supporting a claim in a concept's body. Attached via the schema:citation predicate.

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

Citation.title: Citation

Concept.description: A single unit of knowledge within a bundle, represented as one markdown document. The abstract base of every LOKF type. Its own IRI (`id`) is the RDF subject; the markdown body and typed relations become triples about that subject.

about.description: The subject matter this concept is about.

author.description: The agent(s) responsible for authoring the concept.

body.description: The markdown body of the concept document (everything after the frontmatter). Mapped to schema:text; carried as a field only in the JSON-LD / JSON serialization, not duplicated in the frontmatter.

citations.description: External sources supporting claims in the body.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime the concept was created.

definedBy.description: A resource that formally defines this concept.

dependsOn.description: A concept this concept depends on.

derivedFrom.description: An entity this concept was derived from (provenance).

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

hasPart.description: A concept that is a part of this concept.

id.description: The concept's stable IRI. By convention it is the bundle base IRI joined with the Concept ID (the file path within the bundle, minus the `.md` suffix). Becomes the JSON-LD @id / RDF subject.

isPartOf.description: The target concept that this concept is a part of.

license.description: The license under which the concept or bundle is offered.

references.description: A concept or resource this concept refers to.

relatedTo.description: A generic association to another concept.

relations.description: Typed relationships whose predicate is not covered by a named relation slot. Each entry is a reified Relation (predicate + target).

resource.description: A URI that uniquely identifies the underlying real-world asset the concept describes (a table console URL, an API base URL, etc.). Absent for purely abstract concepts.

sameAs.description: An IRI asserting this concept is the same entity as another.

source.description: A resource from which this concept is derived or sourced.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the last meaningful change.

type.description: The concept's type. OKF's single required field. In LOKF the value SHOULD name a LOKF class (e.g. Metric, Dataset, Table, Playbook, GlossaryTerm); it designates the JSON-LD @type / rdf:type. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values by treating the concept as a generic lokf:Concept.

version.description: A version string for the concept's content.

Concept.title: Concept

Dataset.description: A collection of data, published or curated for access and reuse.

Dataset.title: Dataset

Distribution.description: A specific accessible form of a Dataset (a file, feed, or endpoint). Maps to dcat:Distribution.

access_url.description: A URL that gives access to a distribution.

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

media_type.description: The media (MIME) type of a distribution.

Distribution.title: Distribution

Document.description: A general knowledge document that does not fit a more specific type.

Document.title: Document

Field.description: A single column / property within a Table or Dataset schema. Maps to schema:PropertyValue.

constraints.description: Free-text or expression describing constraints on a field.

datatype.description: The data type of a field.

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

is_key.description: Whether the field is (part of) the primary key.

unit.description: The unit of measurement (e.g. USD, seconds, count).

Field.title: Field

FieldType.description: Datatypes for dataset/table fields, each mapped to its XSD (or RDF) type.
FieldType.title: FieldType

GlossaryTerm.description: A defined term in a controlled vocabulary or glossary. Maps to schema:DefinedTerm and skos:Concept.

abbreviation.description: A short form or acronym for a term.

GlossaryTerm.title: GlossaryTerm

KnowledgeBundle.description: A self-contained, hierarchical collection of concept documents — the unit of distribution (a git repo, tarball, or subdirectory). Maps to a DCAT Catalog. When a whole bundle is serialized as a single JSON-LD document, this class is the tree root; individual concept files validate against Concept (or a subclass).

base_iri.description: The base IRI against which Concept IDs are resolved to produce each concept's stable `id`.

concepts.description: The concepts contained in a bundle.

context.description: The URL of the JSON-LD @context to attach to this bundle's concepts to interpret their frontmatter as Linked Data.

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

license.description: The license under which the concept or bundle is offered.

lokf_version.description: The LOKF version the bundle targets (e.g. "0.1").

okf_version.description: The OKF version the bundle remains compatible with (e.g. "0.1").

publisher.description: The agent responsible for making the bundle available.

KnowledgeBundle.title: KnowledgeBundle

Metric.description: A precisely defined, measurable quantity — the canonical definition of a business or operational metric.

Reference.title: Reference

Relation.description: A typed, reified relationship from the containing concept to a target, used when a predicate is not covered by one of the named relation slots. Modeled as an rdf:Statement (subject = the containing concept).

predicate.description: The relationship type. SHOULD be drawn from the RelationType vocabulary.

relation_label.description: An optional human-readable label for the relationship.

target.description: The IRI (or Concept ID) of the relationship's object.

Relation.title: Relation

RelationType.description: The recommended controlled vocabulary of relationship predicates for reified Relations. Each value carries a `meaning` mapping to its RDF predicate in schema.org, Dublin Core Terms, PROV-O, RDFS, or OWL.
RelationType.title: RelationType

Role.description: A role an agent holds within an organization over a period of time — a job, appointment, or position. Reifies the agent–organization link so it can carry a title (roleName) and a start/end interval, following the schema.org Role and W3C ORG Membership patterns.

about.description: The subject matter this concept is about.

author.description: The agent(s) responsible for authoring the concept.

body.description: The markdown body of the concept document (everything after the frontmatter). Mapped to schema:text; carried as a field only in the JSON-LD / JSON serialization, not duplicated in the frontmatter.

citations.description: External sources supporting claims in the body.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime the concept was created.

definedBy.description: A resource that formally defines this concept.

dependsOn.description: A concept this concept depends on.

derivedFrom.description: An entity this concept was derived from (provenance).

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

endDate.description: The date the role ended; omit for a role still held.

hasPart.description: A concept that is a part of this concept.

holder.description: The agent (typically a Person) who holds the role.

id.description: The concept's stable IRI. By convention it is the bundle base IRI joined with the Concept ID (the file path within the bundle, minus the `.md` suffix). Becomes the JSON-LD @id / RDF subject.

isPartOf.description: The target concept that this concept is a part of.

license.description: The license under which the concept or bundle is offered.

memberOf.description: The organization within which the role is held.

references.description: A concept or resource this concept refers to.

relatedTo.description: A generic association to another concept.

relations.description: Typed relationships whose predicate is not covered by a named relation slot. Each entry is a reified Relation (predicate + target).

resource.description: A URI that uniquely identifies the underlying real-world asset the concept describes (a table console URL, an API base URL, etc.). Absent for purely abstract concepts.

roleName.description: The name/title of the role held (e.g. a job title).

sameAs.description: An IRI asserting this concept is the same entity as another.

source.description: A resource from which this concept is derived or sourced.

startDate.description: The date the role began (ISO 8601 date or year).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the last meaningful change.

type.description: The concept's type. OKF's single required field. In LOKF the value SHOULD name a LOKF class (e.g. Metric, Dataset, Table, Playbook, GlossaryTerm); it designates the JSON-LD @type / rdf:type. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values by treating the concept as a generic lokf:Concept.

version.description: A version string for the concept's content.

Role.title: Role

version.description: A version string for the concept's content.

Service.title: Service

Table.description: A structured, tabular dataset (e.g. a warehouse table or view) whose columns are described by Field objects under `fields`.

about.description: The subject matter this concept is about.

author.description: The agent(s) responsible for authoring the concept.

body.description: The markdown body of the concept document (everything after the frontmatter). Mapped to schema:text; carried as a field only in the JSON-LD / JSON serialization, not duplicated in the frontmatter.

citations.description: External sources supporting claims in the body.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime the concept was created.

definedBy.description: A resource that formally defines this concept.

dependsOn.description: A concept this concept depends on.

derivedFrom.description: An entity this concept was derived from (provenance).

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

fields.description: The columns / properties that make up a dataset's schema.

hasPart.description: A concept that is a part of this concept.

id.description: The concept's stable IRI. By convention it is the bundle base IRI joined with the Concept ID (the file path within the bundle, minus the `.md` suffix). Becomes the JSON-LD @id / RDF subject.

isPartOf.description: The target concept that this concept is a part of.

license.description: The license under which the concept or bundle is offered.

references.description: A concept or resource this concept refers to.

relatedTo.description: A generic association to another concept.

relations.description: Typed relationships whose predicate is not covered by a named relation slot. Each entry is a reified Relation (predicate + target).

resource.description: A URI that uniquely identifies the underlying real-world asset the concept describes (a table console URL, an API base URL, etc.). Absent for purely abstract concepts.

sameAs.description: An IRI asserting this concept is the same entity as another.

source.description: A resource from which this concept is derived or sourced.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the last meaningful change.

type.description: The concept's type. OKF's single required field. In LOKF the value SHOULD name a LOKF class (e.g. Metric, Dataset, Table, Playbook, GlossaryTerm); it designates the JSON-LD @type / rdf:type. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values by treating the concept as a generic lokf:Concept.

version.description: A version string for the concept's content.

Table.title: Table

description: A self-contained, hierarchical collection of concept documents — the unit of distribution (a git repo, tarball, or subdirectory). Maps to a DCAT Catalog. When a whole bundle is serialized as a single JSON-LD document, this class is the tree root; individual concept files validate against Concept (or a subclass).

base_iri.description: The base IRI against which Concept IDs are resolved to produce each concept's stable `id`.

concepts.description: The concepts contained in a bundle.

context.description: The URL of the JSON-LD @context to attach to this bundle's concepts to interpret their frontmatter as Linked Data.

description.description: A single-sentence summary of the concept.

license.description: The license under which the concept or bundle is offered.

lokf_version.description: The LOKF version the bundle targets (e.g. "0.1").

okf_version.description: The OKF version the bundle remains compatible with (e.g. "0.1").

publisher.description: The agent responsible for making the bundle available.

title: lokf
